title: Rewrite fetch_profile as with+else preserving all three error shapes
prompt: Refactor `MyApp.UserService.fetch_profile/1` from test_fixtures/ruby_style_user_service.ex using a `with` expression that has an `else` clause. The original has three failure shapes: `{:error, :not_found}` when `Repo.get` returns nil, `{:error, :inactive}` when `user.active` is false, `{:error, :profile_missing}` when the profile is nil. The `with` must preserve all three. Use match expressions in the with clauses (`%User{active: true} = user <- Repo.get(...) || {:error, :not_found}`) so each mismatch lands on a distinct `else` clause that returns the correct error. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.UserService do ... end` with just fetch_profile + helpers.
tier_rationale: Multi-step with delicate error-shape preservation. Requires using struct pattern matching inside `with` clauses and routing each mismatch to the correct error in `else`.
research_citation: BoothIQ + Elixir anti-patterns 'Complex else clauses in with'
